reflectionSymmetryStatus.label: full-line reflection symmetry
reflectionSymmetryStatus.reason: The Weyl kernel satisfies K(a,b)=K(-a,-b), so parity sectors are invariant.

halfLineParityReductionStatus.label: half-line parity reduction
halfLineParityReductionStatus.reason: Even and odd extensions identify the full-line quadratic form with the two half-line forms A+B and A-B.

contractionFormStatus.label: A/B contraction formulation
contractionFormStatus.reason: Positivity of both parity sectors is equivalent to A>=0 and -A<=B<=A.

proof: Decompose full-line tests into even and odd parts.
Use reflection symmetry to make the even/odd sectors orthogonal for the form.
Transport each sector to the half-line to get kernels A+B and A-B.
Rewrite positivity of both sectors as the A/B contraction inequality.
